docs.title: AppExpress – Offer Pre-Launch
docs.description: Your apps, ready-to-use, à la carte.
docs.content:
### AppExpress – Offer Pre-Launch The **pre-release version of the AppExpress offer** is now live. This is a first iteration of the offer as I envision it: clear, readable, and execution-focused, ready to be used today to structure a project. The goal is simple: **reduce the distance between an idea and production.**
The platform already allows you to: - build an offer from explicit building blocks - get a budget and timeline estimate - book a meeting - or subscribe to the newsletter to follow the evolution of the offer The estimate serves as a basis for discussion. The final proposal is validated after an exchange, but the starting point is intentionally clear. The offer is built on a few deliberate principles: - a single point of contact from start to finish - zero outsourcing - a pragmatic, delivery-oriented approach - the option to start with or without designs Mobile, web, SaaS, or generative AI: the objective is always the same, **deliver a functional product**. --- ### Next steps - **Two demo projects** to concretely illustrate the offer and the building blocks used. - **The AppExpress CRM**, allowing project progress tracking, discussions, and milestone validation, accessible via a web dashboard and a mobile app. The mobile app is built with Flutter, delivering a native experience on both iOS and Android from a single codebase. - **More generative AI–oriented projects**, to explore and demonstrate concrete use cases: **intelligent search, content generation, assistants, and automations.**

docs.title: LLMVoice 1.0
docs.description: Technical demo designed to evaluate the new speech recognition APIs of iOS 26 (SpeechAnalyzer / SpeechTranscriber) and the execution of open-weight LLM models (quantized in 4 bits) locally on an iPhone supporting the MLX framework.
docs.content:
Here is **LLMVoice**, a technical demo designed to evaluate the new iOS 26 speech recognition APIs (**SpeechAnalyzer / SpeechTranscriber**) and the execution of **open-weight LLM models** (quantized in **4 bits**) locally on an iPhone that supports the **MLX** framework. You can either summarize a transcription or send a prompt directly to the model. Let’s be honest — models this compressed aren’t particularly useful, but it’s still fun to watch them hallucinate. That said, they’re powerful enough to bring my old iPhone 13 to its knees! Here are the available downloadable models:
**Gemma3_1b** (mlx-community/gemma-3-1b-it-4bit)
**Qwen25_05b** (lmstudio-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-MLX-4bit)
**Llama32_1b** (mlx-community/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-4bit)
## Source Code ## System Requirements iOS 26.0+ / iPadOS 26.0+ ### For Apple Intelligence iPhone 15 Pro or later Apple Intelligence enabled in Settings ### For local MLX models iPhone 12 or later (A14 Bionic chip or higher).
iPad Pro 2021 or later (M1 chip or higher).
iPad Air 5th generation or later (M1 chip or higher).
Metal GPU with specific features: air.simd_sum, rmsfloat16 kernel support.
### Storage App size: about 50 MB Model downloads (MLX only): Qwen2.5 (0.5B): about 150 MB
Gemma 3 (1B): about 300 MB
Llama 3.2 (1B): about 500 MB

--- ## 🛟 Support Need help or want to share feedback? Email [support@andrefrelicot.dev](mailto:support@andrefrelicot.dev).

docs.title: BLE UScope 1.0
docs.description: Advanced Bluetooth Low Energy scanner and analyzer for iOS, iPadOS, Android, and macOS Catalyst.
docs.content:
<ThemeImage
  lightSrc="/images/2025/ble-uscope.webp"
  darkSrc="/images/2025/ble-uscope.webp"
  alt="BLE UScope on iPhone"
  width={800}
  height={600}
  priority={true}
/>

# **BLE UScope – Advanced Bluetooth Low Energy Scanner and Analyzer**

BLE UScope is a Bluetooth Low Energy analysis tool for iOS, iPadOS, Android, and macOS Catalyst. The app scans nearby peripherals, reads advertisement data, tracks RSSI, then connects to explore their GATT structure.

Its goal is to make everyday BLE work more direct: inspect services and characteristics, read or write values, enable notifications, follow connection events, record monitoring sessions, and export usable traces.

## Demos

<YouTubeEmbed
  videoId="n_EJOZfy4vo"
  title="BLE UScope demo"
  caption="Unlisted BLE UScope demo showing scanning and device exploration."
/>

<YouTubeEmbed
  videoId="XsccrDK7ITg"
  title="BLE UScope additional demo"
  caption="Second unlisted BLE UScope demo focused on analyzing a connected device."
/>

## From scanning to GATT

The first screen is about observing the BLE environment in real time. BLE UScope shows discovered devices with RSSI, advertisement data, advertised services when available, and an estimated device category based on service UUIDs, manufacturer data, or the device name.

Once connected, the app moves into a more diagnostic-oriented detail view. You can browse services, characteristics, and descriptors, filter readable, writable, or notifiable characteristics, then inspect values as hexadecimal, UTF-8, or decimal data depending on the case. GATT properties stay visible, so you do not have to guess whether a characteristic supports `read`, `write`, `notify`, or `indicate`.

This part intentionally stays close to BLE terminology. When inspecting a peripheral, you need UUIDs, raw values, descriptors, and notification state to remain visible instead of being hidden behind a simplified UI.

## Screenshots

<ScreenshotGallery
  images={[
    {
      src: "/images/2025/ble-uscope/ble-uscope-ios.webp",
      alt: "BLE UScope on iPhone with a BLE device detail view",
      width: 720,
      height: 1485,
    },
    {
      src: "/images/2025/ble-uscope/ble-uscope-android.webp",
      alt: "BLE UScope on Android scanning BLE devices",
      width: 720,
      height: 1516,
    },
    {
      src: "/images/2025/ble-uscope/ble-uscope-ipad.webp",
      alt: "BLE UScope on iPad with a multi-column layout",
      width: 1400,
      height: 1011,
    },
    {
      src: "/images/2025/ble-uscope/ble-uscope-macos.webp",
      alt: "BLE UScope on macOS Catalyst",
      width: 1400,
      height: 925,
    },
  ]}
  caption="BLE UScope 1.0.1 screenshots on iPhone, Android, iPad, and macOS Catalyst."
/>

## Monitoring, diagnostics, and exports

Scanning is only one part of the workflow. BLE UScope also lets you follow a connection over time: signal quality, connection duration, RSSI history, connection events, and received notifications.

BLE notifications can be recorded into monitoring sessions. This is useful when a characteristic emits a measurement, a state update, or a domain-specific event at a regular cadence. The app keeps values with their timestamp, device, service, characteristic, and RSSI when that information is available.

Diagnostics help validate a connected peripheral: service discovery, connection stability, characteristic reads, and simple response or throughput checks. Exports make it possible to keep a record of what was observed: device profile, GATT structure, characteristic values, logs, or notification sessions.

## Implementation

The Apple version is built with SwiftUI and CoreBluetooth. It supports iOS, iPadOS, and macOS through Catalyst, with layouts adapted to each screen size. The BLE layer handles scanning, multiple connections, CoreBluetooth restoration, reconnects, GATT exploration, and notification monitoring.

The Android version is built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose. It uses `BluetoothGatt`, a sequential operation queue to avoid BLE operation collisions, state flows to synchronize the UI, Room for local data, and dedicated views for logs, favorites, history, and diagnostics.

Both versions share the same product intent while respecting each platform's constraints. On Android, that means dealing with modern Bluetooth permissions, scan throttling, and device-specific BLE stack behavior. On iOS, CoreBluetooth defines its own restoration, authorization, and lifecycle model.

## Data and privacy

BLE UScope runs locally. There is no account, no analytics, and no BLE data sent to a server. Favorites, custom names, histories, logs, and exports stay on the device unless you explicitly share or export them.

## Download

Available on the App Store and Google Play.

<AppStoreLink href="https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ble-uscope/id6753627148" />
<PlayStoreLink href="https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=dev.andrefrelicot.bleuscope" />

---

## Support

Need help or want to share feedback? Email [support@andrefrelicot.dev](mailto:support@andrefrelicot.dev).

docs.title: DecibelPeak
docs.description: DecibelPeak is a mobile sound level meter for iOS and Android with real-time decibel readings, audio visualizations, history, dB peak tracking, and local calibration.
docs.content:
# Real-time sound levels with visual context DecibelPeak starts from a simple idea: open the app, allow microphone access, and immediately see the sound level around you. The current version keeps that direct reading, then adds more context: a circular gauge, waveform, spectrum, FFT bars, FFT circle, waterfall, dB curve, and dB peak view. It is not meant to replace a certified laboratory sound meter, but it is useful for observing an acoustic environment, comparing two situations, and keeping a real-time visual reference. ## What the app shows The main view combines an instant dB value, a graduated gauge, and a sound-zone indicator. Levels are grouped into readable ranges, from quiet to loud, so the app does not leave you with a number alone. The top area can cycle through several audio visualizations. The fast views show the waveform and spectrum, while the FFT and waterfall modes provide a more frequency-oriented look. The dB curve and dB peak modes are better suited for understanding changes over time: a gradual rise, a short event, the session average, or the highest value reached. Local calibration lets you adjust the measurement offset in 0.5 dB steps. That helps bring DecibelPeak closer to a reference device or compensate for differences between phone microphones. ## Screenshots ## Implementation The iOS version is built with SwiftUI, AVFoundation for audio capture, and Accelerate for frequency analysis. The Android version follows the same product direction with Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, and AudioRecord. Processing happens on device. Audio is analyzed in real time to compute the sound level, feed the visualizations, and update the history. DecibelPeak is not designed to record conversations: it works from the live signal and does not send audio data to a server. ## Source code ## Download Available on the App Store and Google Play.
--- ## Support Need help or want to share feedback? Write to [support@andrefrelicot.dev](mailto:support@andrefrelicot.dev).

docs.title: Hello World!
docs.description: Welcome to my Devlog!  cyber • infosec • cloud • IoT • GPU • graphics • XR • mobile • music & audio
docs.content:
<ThemeImage
  lightSrc="/images/2025/hello-world.webp"
  darkSrc="/images/2025/hello-world.webp"
  alt="Where everything is built"
  width={800}
  height={600}
  priority={true}
/>

# Welcome to my Devlog!  

I’ll be sharing here my journey and explorations of new technologies, with the goal of creating and distributing new products.  

I’m starting by developing *showcase* apps, both mobile and web. Commercial apps will follow in the coming months.  

My main areas of interest are:  
- **mobile**  
- **audio** (DSP, VST/AU)  
- **music** (MIDI, theory)  
- **graphics** (3D, GPU, visualization, shaders)  
- **VR/XR** (Meta Quest, Apple Vision)  
- the **cybersphere** in general (social networks, virality, IoT, etc.)  
- **generative AI**  
- **Web 3.0** (blockchain, cryptos). 

I’m also interested in creating **mini video games**, whether based on original ideas or adaptations.  

In the long run, I’ll operate under different banners:  
- **Osibits** for commercial distribution and freelancing  
- another entity for software production  
- a third one for content creation (sound, music, images, videos, 3D VFX, etc.)  

---

**Stay tuned!**

docs.title: About
docs.description: André Frélicot - TechLead, Entrepreneur, Digital Strategist. Tech - Product - Business.
docs.content:
# About I've been a software developer (CTO, Architect, Tech Lead) for over 25 years, working in companies, startups, and as a freelancer. I've contributed to a wide range of projects: management software, mobile apps integrating machine learning and video streaming, medical applications requiring advanced visualization systems (MedTech, DeepTech, distributed volume rendering, 4D Flow, GPU clusters in the cloud), as well as mobile apps using real-time Bluetooth LE communication (including firmware development for BLE peripherals). In 2025, I'm starting a new chapter: exploring the opportunities offered by AI (LLMs, deep learning) to create new types of products. While remaining deeply rooted in technology, I'm also interested in new ways software is consumed: virality, customer acquisition through social media (UGC), and marketing strategies. Where software merges with content. My role is evolving into that of a **Digital Strategist**, envisioning, distributing, and selling both mobile applications and SaaS solutions. **Motto: Tech – Product – Business** --- # Freelancing & Consulting **I help businesses turn their ideas into high-performing mobile and web applications.** I remain available for contract work. My focus is on iOS mobile app development (**SwiftUI**), Android (**Kotlin, Jetpack Compose**) and cross-platform apps (**React Native, Flutter, Ionic**), as well as modern web applications (**React / Next.js**), deployed on high-performance cloud solutions (**Vercel, CloudFlare, AWS, Convex, Supabase, Neon, Firebase**). My hourly rate is **$90/h**. 📩 Contact: **andre@frelicot.com** --- # Company **Osibits**
60 rue François 1er
75008 Paris
